stories.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
stories.simple_summary: Meta (Facebook's parent company) released a new AI tool that helps computer programmers write code faster and fix mistakes. It's designed to handle big, complicated projects.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to large businesses and programmers — nothing you need to worry about as a small-business owner unless you hire programmers.

stories.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
stories.simple_summary: The New York Times says OpenAI didn't show them tools that could prove ChatGPT copied their news articles. They're suing over this.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal battle between big companies and lawyers — it won't directly affect you, though it may eventually affect what AI can and can't do with content.

stories.title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
stories.simple_summary: Google now requires advertisers to tell people when an ad was created or changed using AI. Before, only election ads had to do this.
stories.why_it_matters: If you run ads online, you'll need to disclose when AI helped create them — but this is a straightforward labeling requirement, not complicated.

stories.title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
stories.simple_summary: A Paris AI company raised $100 million to expand to California and compete with other AI companies. This is mostly about investors and where tech jobs are growing.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters to investors and tech workers — nothing relevant to ordinary people or most small businesses.

stories.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
stories.simple_summary: It's unclear exactly how the government decided it was safe for OpenAI to release its latest powerful AI system.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly a question for policy experts and investors — it doesn't directly affect how you'd use AI day-to-day.

stories.title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
stories.simple_summary: Meta's AI can now generate pictures using photos from anyone's public Instagram account. You can now opt out and prevent your photos from being used this way.
stories.why_it_matters: If you have a public Instagram account, you should check this setting if you don't want your photos used to train Meta's AI image generator.

stories.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI announced a new very powerful AI model.
stories.why_it_matters: This is exciting news for tech companies using cutting-edge AI, but ordinary people should wait to see what it actually does before worrying about it.

stories.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
stories.simple_summary: Meta is building its own computer chips designed for AI, and they'll start making them in September. The design can be changed if AI technology shifts quickly.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters to investors and engineers — it shows Meta is serious about AI — but it won't directly affect your daily life.

stories.title: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
stories.simple_summary: Nvidia sells the powerful computer chips that everyone uses for AI, but because so many companies want to buy them, other businesses are getting rich selling simpler products around the edges.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly an investors' story — nothing you need to do or understand unless you own stock.

stories.title: Microsoft’s patch Tuesdays are about to get bigger
stories.simple_summary: Microsoft is using AI to find Windows security problems faster, so Windows 11 will get more security updates at once. This helps protect against hackers who are also using AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is good news — your Windows computer will get better security protection without you doing anything different.

stories.title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6 after government greenlight — and announces ‘ChatGPT Work’
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.6 (a very powerful AI) to the public after government approval. They also launched ChatGPT Work, a version for businesses.
stories.why_it_matters: There's now a business version of ChatGPT available if your company wants to use AI — but it's optional and you'd need to decide if it fits your needs.

stories.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic (a company that makes Claude, a chatbot) added a dashboard that shows you how much you use their AI. The dashboard subtly encourages you to use it even more.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Claude, be aware they're tracking and highlighting your usage — it's a marketing technique to get you hooked on the tool.
